headline: DAO can reset crvUSD minting policy via Aragon vote; no separate post-vote timelock observed

findings.text: crvUSD token owner() resolves to the Curve DAO Ownership Agent (0x4090…), and the token exposes monetary_policy and set_monetary_policy; mint() is gated by monetary_policy.
ControllerFactory owner() resolves to the Curve DAO Ownership Agent (0x4090…) and emergency_admin() resolves to the Emergency DAO multisig (0x4679…).
Core crvUSD token and ControllerFactory appear as non-proxy contracts on Etherscan; governance stack uses Aragon Voting and an Agent app (Aragon app proxies) to execute calls.
Execution path is Voting (0xE478…) → Ownership Agent (0x4090…) → target contract (e.g., crvUSD token or ControllerFactory); no separate timelock executor contract observed on this path.
Emergency DAO multisig is a Gnosis Safe at 0x4679… with getThreshold() = 5, indicating 5-of-9; it is set as emergency_admin on ControllerFactory.
On-chain governance is Aragon Voting at 0xE478…; proposal/voting parameters (voteTime, quorum, support) were not read in this run.
ControllerFactory exposes an emergency_admin role (set to the Emergency DAO multisig), indicating separate emergency powers from the main DAO owner.
T1 fund-critical power exists: the DAO, via the Ownership Agent, can call set_monetary_policy on the crvUSD token, which controls who can mint via mint() gated by monetary_policy.

steelman.red: If a small multisig or EOA directly held the crvUSD owner or could bypass governance, they could instantly set monetary_policy and mint unbacked crvUSD with no delay.
steelman.orange: The DAO controls a T1 surface (crvUSD mint authority) via Aragon Voting/Agent with no separate timelock executor, and an emergency 5-of-9 multisig has fast emergency powers on the factory—parameters and voting delays appear shorter than seven days or are unverified.
steelman.green: Core contracts (token/factory) are non-proxy and governed on-chain via token voting; changes require passing a vote and there is no single-signer control, providing a non-instant path for T1 actions.

rationale.verdict: Choosing orange because a T1 path exists: the crvUSD token’s minting authority is controlled by set_monetary_policy, and owner() resolves to the DAO’s Ownership Agent; execution flows through Aragon Voting and the Agent with no separate post-queue timelock (only the voting period), and an emergency 5-of-9 multisig is set as emergency_admin on the factory (Etherscan: crvUSD token code/reads; ControllerFactory reads; Voting/Agent code; Emergency DAO threshold). The exact voting delay/quorum were not read in this run, so we conservatively treat the uncontested-fast-path delay as <7 days or unknown on a T1 surface.

evidence.shows: crvUSD token Read Contract; owner() returns the Curve DAO Ownership Agent address; exposes monetary_policy() read.
crvUSD token source shows monetary_policy variable, set_monetary_policy (owner-only), and mint() restricted to monetary_policy.
crvUSD ControllerFactory Read Contract; owner() set to the Ownership Agent; emergency_admin() set to the Emergency DAO multisig.
ControllerFactory source indicating owner/admin and emergency_admin roles (emergency controls).
Curve DAO Voting contract (Aragon Voting app) used for on-chain governance proposals and execution eligibility.
Curve DAO Ownership Agent (Aragon Agent app) that executes calls on owned contracts when governance votes pass.
Emergency DAO multisig (Gnosis Safe) Read Contract; getThreshold() returns 5 (5-of-9).
CRV governance token contract on Ethereum (for protocol governance ecosystem context).
Audit report describing crvUSD architecture (stablecoin, controllers, LLAMMA) supporting the protocol description.

unknowns: C1: Did not read owners/admins for all per-market Controllers and AMM/LLAMMA instances to confirm newest-version control surfaces.
C2: Could not determine the specific proxy admin/upgrade authority for the Aragon apps (Voting/Agent) from on-chain reads during this run.
C3: Did not read voteTime or any early-execution flags for the Voting app; no explicit queue/execute timelock contract identified beyond Voting.
C4: Emergency DAO signer identities and insider/non-insider classifications are not determinable from on-chain reads; other operations/grants multisigs (if any) were not enumerated.
C5: Proposal threshold, voting period (in seconds), quorum/support parameters for the Voting app were not read on-chain in this run.
C6: Did not exhaustively confirm whether emergency_admin or other guardians have any direct T1 powers on token/controllers beyond documented emergency/parameter scopes.

admin_addresses.role: Curve DAO Ownership Agent (executor of passed votes)
Emergency DAO multisig (5-of-9) — emergency_admin on factory

protocol_metadata.about: crvUSD is a collateralized debt position (CDP) stablecoin issued by Curve. Users borrow crvUSD against supported collateral via per-market controllers and a banded AMM mechanism (LLAMMA) designed to soften liquidations. Governance (via an Aragon Voting/Agent stack) controls parameters and the token’s minting authority, while an Emergency DAO multisig holds scoped emergency powers on certain factories.
